title: PutObject, Streaming Bodies, Metadata, and Checksums · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for putobject, streaming bodies, metadata, and checksums and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for TypeScript single-object integrity record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, own and close input bodies, preserve cancellation, bound bytes, and distinguish request completion from later business publication.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, select supported checksum algorithms, send full-object evidence, validate service response, and retain an application integrity record.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set and preserve Content-Type, Content-Disposition, Cache-Control, encoding, language, custom metadata, and tags during replacements and copies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3145 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to verify PutObject bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without ETag overclaim; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, own and close input bodies, preserve cancellation, bound bytes, and distinguish request completion from later business publication.
